revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Kulturgutschutzgesetz (Japan)]][[ja:文化財保護法]]
{{Japan law |title=The Cultural Properties Protection Law |nickname= |number=Law No. 214 of Shōwa 25 (1950) |status=In force |type=[[Education Law]] |jurisdiction=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |content=Protection of [[:en:Cultural properties|Cultural properties]] |related=[[Museum Act]], [[Landscape Act]], [[Urban Planning Act]], [[Act on the Maintenance and Improvement of Historic Landscapes in Local Areas|Historic Townscape Act]], [[Basic Act on Culture and Arts]], [[Act on the Promotion of Cultural Tourism at Regional Core Facilities|Cultural Tourism Promotion Act]] |link=[https://laws.e-gov.go.jp/document?lawid=325AC0100000214 e-Gov Law] |Wikisource=Cultural Properties Protection Law }} '''Cultural Properties Protection Law''' (文化財保護法, ''Bunkazai Hogo-hō'') is a {{ill|Japanese|en|Japanese|qid=Q346080|lt=Japanese|1=Japanese}} {{ill|Law|en|Law|qid=Q7748|lt=Law|1=Law}} enacted on [[May 30, 1950]] (Law No. 214 of {{ill|Shōwa|en|Shōwa|qid=Q398996|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa}} 25), which aims to preserve and utilize [[:en:Cultural properties|Cultural properties]] while promoting the [[:en:cultural|cultural]] development of the {{ill|Nation|en|Nation|qid=Q6266|lt=Nation|1=Nation}}. The law classifies cultural properties into {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q2901860|lt=tangible|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}} and [[:en:Intangible Cultural Properties|intangible]] categories. Based on their importance, the designation, selection, determination, certification, or registration is performed by the {{ill|Minister of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|en|Minister of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|qid=Q842359|lt=Minister of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|1=Minister of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology}} or the [[Commissioner for Cultural Affairs]] at the national level, [[Prefecture governor]] at the prefectural level, or {{ill|Mayor|en|Mayor|qid=Q30185|lt=Mayor|1=Mayor}} at the municipal level. Partial expenses for the protection of these properties can be covered by public funds. Cultural properties are broadly categorized into tangible cultural properties, such as buildings, paintings, sculptures, crafts, tools ([[national treasures]] and [[:en:Important Cultural Properties|Important Cultural Properties]]), and intangible cultural properties, such as [[traditional crafts]] and skilled individuals ([[Living National Treasures]]), as well as [[folk cultural properties]] (festivals, local traditions, performing arts).<ref name="Yomiuri20210419">[Scanner] Protecting Intangible Cultural Properties with a New "Registration" System to Support Cultural Succession Amid Population Aging and Decline, ''{{ill|Yomiuri Shimbun|en|Yomiuri Shimbun|qid=Q645218|lt=Yomiuri Shimbun|1=The Yomiuri Shimbun}}'' Morning Edition, April 19, 2021, p.3</ref> The law divides administrative responsibilities between the [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]]' Cultural Properties Department, with the first section handling intangible cultural properties and the second section handling tangible ones. == History of Enactment and Revisions == On January 26, 1949, a fire broke out at the {{ill|Golden Hall|en|Golden Hall|qid=Q11124250|lt=Golden Hall|1=Golden Hall}} of [[Hōryū-ji]] in {{ill|Ikaruga|en|Ikaruga|qid=Q1658068|lt=Ikaruga|1=Ikaruga}}, [[Ikoma District]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, destroying the [[Hōryū-ji Wall Paintings]]. This incident prompted the enactment of this law through [[legislation proposed by members of the National Diet]] to serve as comprehensive legislation for the protection of cultural properties.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkashingikai/bunkazai/sekaitokubetsu/01/sanko_4_1.html|title=Overview of Cultural Property Protection System|publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]]|date=2006-09-26|accessdate=2024-08-08|qq=}}</ref>{{main|Hōryū-ji Golden Hall Wall Paintings#Fire Damage|qq=}} The enforcement date of the Cultural Properties Protection Law was set by [[Cabinet Order]] No. 276 of Shōwa 25, which came into effect on August 29, 1950. Upon enforcement, its predecessors—the [[Historic Sites, Scenic Beauty and Natural Monuments Preservation Act]] (enacted in 1919), the [[National Treasures Preservation Law]] (enacted in 1929), and the [[Important Art Objects Preservation Act]] (enacted in 1933)—were abolished. In the amendment passed on April 16, 2021, a new registration system was established for intangible cultural properties, allowing for public funding support while having less stringent regulations. This aims to protect cultural activities affected by declining birth rates, aging populations, and depopulation.<ref name="Yomiuri20210419"/> == Structure == {{For2|Cultural properties classifications under this law|Cultural Properties|qq=}} * Chapter 1: General Provisions (Articles 1–4) * Chapter 2: Deleted * Chapter 3: {{ill|Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q2901860|lt=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|1=Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)}} ''' Section 1: [[:en:Important Cultural Properties|Important Cultural Properties]] '''* Subsection 1: Designation (Articles 27–29) '''* Subsection 2: Management (Articles 30–34) '''* Subsection 3: Protection (Articles 34-2–47) '''* Subsection 4: Public Access (Articles 47-2–53) '''* Subsection 5: Investigation (Articles 54–55) '''* Subsection 6: Miscellaneous (Article 56) ''' Section 2: [[Registered Tangible Cultural Properties]] (Articles 57–69) ''' Section 3: Tangible Cultural Properties other than Important and Registered Cultural Properties (Article 70) * Chapter 4: [[:en:Intangible Cultural Properties|Intangible Cultural Properties]] (Articles 71–77) * Chapter 5: [[Folk Cultural Properties]] (Articles 78–91) * Chapter 6: [[:en:Buried Cultural Properties|Buried Cultural Properties]] (Articles 92–108) * Chapter 7: [[:en:Historic Sites|Historic Sites]], [[:en:Places of Scenic Beauty|Places of Scenic Beauty]], and [[:en:Natural Monuments|Natural Monuments]] (Articles 109–133) * Chapter 8: [[:en:Important Cultural Landscapes|Important Cultural Landscapes]] (Articles 134–141) * Chapter 9: [[Traditional Building Preservation Districts]] (Articles 142–146) * Chapter 10: Protection of Cultural Property Preservation Techniques (Articles 147–152) * Chapter 11: Consultations with the {{ill|Council for Cultural Affairs|en|Council for Cultural Affairs|qid=Q111244755|lt=Council for Cultural Affairs|1=Council for Cultural Affairs}} (Article 153) * Chapter 12: Supplementary Provisions ''' Section 1: Hearings, Opinions, and Appeals (Articles 154–161) ''' Section 2: Exceptions Concerning the National Government (Articles 162–181) ''' Section 3: Local Governments and [[:en:Board of Education|Board of Education]] (Articles 182–192) * Chapter 13: Penal Provisions (Articles 193–203) == Notifications == When {{ill|Earthenware|en|Earthenware|qid=Q1377111|lt=Earthenware|1=Earthenware}} or [[:en:Stone tools|Stone tools]] are discovered on private land, the artifacts are considered [[buried objects]] and must be reported to the relevant {{ill|police station|en|police station|qid=Q861951|lt=police station|1=Police station}}. Simultaneously, a report of the archaeological site must be submitted to the Commissioner for Cultural Affairs. Even artifacts that would be designated as cultural properties today, if discovered before the enactment of this law, are permitted for private ownership or trade in the {{ill|antique|en|antique|qid=Q472760|lt=antique|1=Antique}} market.<ref>"Welcome to the Gallery / Encountering Earthenware in Ginza," ''{{ill|Nikkei|en|Nikkei|qid=Q413131|lt=Nikkei|1=Nikkei}}'' Morning Edition, August 27, 2017, p.10, NIKKEI The STYLE</ref> == Notes == {{Footnotes|qq=}} {{reflist|qq=}}
== See Also == *[[:en:Cultural properties|Cultural properties]] *[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] *[[Cultural Heritage Protection System]] *[[List of Cultural Properties Held by the Agency for Cultural Affairs]] *[[List of Educational Laws]] == External Links == * [https://hourei.ndl.go.jp/#/detail?lawId=0000042652 History of the Cultural Properties Protection Law] - Japanese Legal Index, Law History List * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/2963558/5 Official Gazette at the Time of Enactment] - {{ill|National Diet Library|en|National Diet Library|qid=Q477675|lt=National Diet Library|1=National Diet Library}} Digital Collection * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index Database of Nationally Designated Cultural Properties] - [[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/ Cultural Heritage Online] - Agency for Cultural Affairs {{Cultural Properties of Japan|qq=}}
{{Archaeology|qq=}}
